sidebar.recentTooltipEmpty: No recent .cfg — they appear after “Save as…” on export or home.

toast.fullscreen: Fullscreen (F11) is available in the Windows / macOS app
toast.exportDesc: Generate the file and use “Save as…”.

generate.title: Generate by mode
generate.descriptionBefore: Pick a mode and save the result as

hero.lead: Generate, tune, and manage configs for CS 1.6, Half-Life, and other GoldSrc games.

insight.draftHintDefault: Save to disk to add it to history

readiness.title: Environment readiness
readiness.descDesktop: Rough score from catalog, recent files, and local DB — not .cfg quality.
readiness.descWeb: Rough score from catalog, recent files, and import draft — not .cfg quality.
readiness.hint100: Catalog, saves, and database in use — solid workflow.
readiness.hint75desktop: Add profiles or history snapshots to finish the checklist.
readiness.hint75web: Save a .cfg to disk or use the desktop app for profiles and history.
readiness.hint50: Save generated .cfg to the game folder to get a recent-file entry.
readiness.hint25: Wait for the mode catalog, then generate and save a config.
readiness.hint0: Load the mode and preset catalog (check network and Diagnostics).

completeness.title: Config completeness
completeness.descEmpty: Per-section score (settings, binds, net, video, crosshair, etc.) — like the Python app indicator.
completeness.descHasData: Share of filled sections from the current draft (import or last home generation).
completeness.emptyBody: No parsed config: generate .cfg on Home or import a file to see the percentage and bar.
completeness.hintHigh: Most sections covered — add net, sound, and buy as needed in the editor.
completeness.hintMid: Add binds, net CVARs, crosshair, and sensitivity — same idea as v2.
completeness.hintLow: Minimum: generate from a mode or import a .cfg, then fill more sections.

sensitivity.title: Mouse sensitivity
sensitivity.description: cm/360° and compare with other configs

recent.title: Recent configs
recent.emptyHint: Generate a config and save with “Save as…”, or import a file

catalog.title: Data catalogs (GitHub)
catalog.description: On startup the app checks JSON updates from the repo in the background (If-Modified-Since). Here you can sync manually or reload files from disk (see app data paths in Settings).
catalog.syncResult: Checked: {{checked}}, updated: {{updated}}, not modified: {{skipped}}, errors in report: {{errors}}
catalog.desktopOnly: Available in the desktop app for Windows / macOS / Linux.

catalog.title: Data catalog updates
catalog.description: Modes, presets, aliases, and the CVAR reference are fetched from GitHub into catalog_data next to app data. A background check runs at startup; here you can check for updates and download manually.
catalog.moreInDiagnostics: Full environment checks are on the Diagnostics page.
